formations_blocks: Croatia keeping most of the ball
Panama looking to play direct and then advance its lines in Croatia's half
key_matchups: Stanišić vs Panama's right side on the overlap
Modrić attacking a cross from Stanišić
Kovačić vs Carlos Harvey in the foul duel
Yoel Bárcenas' free-kick delivery vs Croatia's aerial defense
phases.summary: Croatia establish early territorial control, circulating possession and getting the first crossing chance through Stanišić on the right. Panama respond with a more direct approach, aiming to bypass pressure and push their lines up once in the attacking half, while the opening minutes are marked by physical duels and set-piece threats rather than sustained chances.

formations_blocks: Panama compact defensive lines / low block
Croatia forced wide by Panama's defensive shape
Panama higher-pressure transition waves / direct breaks
adjustments: Croatia resorting to speculative through balls and crosses due to lack of half-space access
Panama briefly increasing pressure after the hydration-break window
Croatia speeding up progression after Panama's pressure spells
key_matchups: Amir Murillo vs Croatia's left-sided defensive recovery and crossing channels
José Luis Rodríguez vs Dominik Livaković on the crossbar chance
Luka Modrić vs Panama's defensive set-piece resistance
Petar Musa vs Panama's aerial defending
phases.summary: Panama's compact, well-drilled lines deny Croatia access to the half-spaces, forcing them into low-value through balls and wide, speculative crossing. After a couple of direct Panama surges and a major chance off Murillo's service, the game stretches slightly, but Croatia still struggles to create centrally and is repeatedly pushed wide or repelled on set pieces.

formations_blocks: limited chance creation despite crossing volume
adjustments: Croatia made two half-time substitutions: Josko Gvardiol off for Ante Budimir, and Petar Musa off for Andrej Kramaric
the changes were made to address Panama’s threat down Croatia’s flank and Croatia’s ineffective centre-forward play
Croatia’s shape may have shifted slightly after the substitutions
key_matchups: Amir Murillo vs Croatia’s left flank/Josko Gvardiol zone
Ante Budimir vs Panama’s central defenders and goalkeeper
phases.summary: The first half was a stop-start, flank-heavy affair in which both teams relied on crosses but produced almost nothing in the way of clear chances. Croatia reacted at halftime with two changes, one to deal with Panama’s success on the wing and another to replace an ineffective striker, and that freshened the attack enough to unlock the game shortly after the break. A through ball and an early cross created the breakthrough for Budimir, turning a sterile contest into a Croatian lead.

formations_blocks: Croatia in a compact defensive shape, blocking central channels
Panama forcing play wide with looping crosses from wide areas
Panama attacking phase with numbers committed forward after conceding
adjustments: Croatia gumming up the central channels and steering Panama into wide areas
Panama continued to lean on short-corner and wide-cross routines despite limited box targets
After the goal, Panama pushed numbers forward in search of an equalizer
key_matchups: Modrić vs Bárcenas (Bárcenas booked for a shoulder into Modrić)
Croatia defense vs Panama’s wide crossing
phases.summary: Croatia managed the phase by denying Panama access through the middle, forcing them into predictable wide deliveries that rarely became real chances. After Budimir’s 54th-minute opener, Croatia nearly doubled the lead on a direct central break, while Panama’s more urgent push after the goal produced some in-box pressure but still met a crowded Croatian defense and several blocked attempts.

formations_blocks: Croatia first-half possession routine to eat up clock
Panama pushing with sustained right-flank attacks
scrappy endgame with both sides blocking shots and Panama pressing for an equalizer
adjustments: Panama substituted Cecilio Waterman on for Jiovany Ramos to add attacking threat
Croatia substituted Mario Pasalić for Luka Modrić on his 200th cap
Panama later made multiple attacking changes: Azarías Lodoño, Tomás Rodríguez, and Éric Davis on for Fajardo, Bárcenas, and Blackman
Panama shifted away from short corners after wasted deliveries and went more direct on set pieces
key_matchups: Amir Murillo vs the Croatian defensive set-up on the right flank
Carlos Harvey attacking the far post from Rodríguez’s corner
Petar Sucić conceding a late free kick near the box
phases.summary: In the closing stages, Croatia tried to slow the game by returning to its possession rhythm, while Panama kept driving primarily down the right and searched for a late equalizer. The match became increasingly scrappy, with blocked shots, repeated dead balls, and Panama throwing on more attackers, but Croatia’s box held firm and limited clear chances.
